dependencies.description: VTP advertisements are sent only over trunk links. No trunk = no VTP propagation.
dependencies.name: 802.1Q trunk links

dependencies.description: All switches in the VTP domain must share the same domain name to exchange VLAN information.
dependencies.name: Matching VTP domain name

failure_modes.description: A switch with a higher VTP configuration revision number than the production domain is connected to the network. Because VTP servers and clients accept the VLAN database from the highest revision number, the new switch's VLAN database (which may be empty, from a lab, or outdated) overwrites the ENTIRE production VLAN database on ALL switches in the VTP domain. Every non-default VLAN is deleted network-wide, causing a catastrophic outage. This is one of the most notorious operational disasters in switching.

resolutions.action: IMMEDIATE: If caught in progress, physically disconnect the offending switch immediately
IMMEDIATE: If caught in progress, physically disconnect the offending switch immediately
IMMEDIATE: If caught in progress, physically disconnect the offending switch immediately
RECOVERY: Re-create all deleted VLANs manually on a VTP server switch — use backup configs or documentation
RECOVERY: Re-create all deleted VLANs manually on a VTP server switch — use backup configs or documentation
RECOVERY: Re-create all deleted VLANs manually on a VTP server switch — use backup configs or documentation
RECOVERY: If VLAN names/IDs are unknown, check access switch configs — 'switchport access vlan' and 'switchport trunk allowed vlan' still reference the VLANs even though they no longer exist
RECOVERY: If VLAN names/IDs are unknown, check access switch configs — 'switchport access vlan' and 'switchport trunk allowed vlan' still reference the VLANs even though they no longer exist
RECOVERY: If VLAN names/IDs are unknown, check access switch configs — 'switchport access vlan' and 'switchport trunk allowed vlan' still reference the VLANs even though they no longer exist
PREVENTION: Use VTP transparent mode on all switches — manually manage VLANs per switch
PREVENTION: Use VTP transparent mode on all switches — manually manage VLANs per switch
PREVENTION: Use VTP transparent mode on all switches — manually manage VLANs per switch
PREVENTION: Upgrade to VTPv3 which requires a primary server to make changes and supports confirmation prompts
PREVENTION: Upgrade to VTPv3 which requires a primary server to make changes and supports confirmation prompts
PREVENTION: Upgrade to VTPv3 which requires a primary server to make changes and supports confirmation prompts
PREVENTION: Reset VTP revision number before connecting any switch: change mode to transparent and back, or change VTP domain name and back
PREVENTION: Reset VTP revision number before connecting any switch: change mode to transparent and back, or change VTP domain name and back
PREVENTION: Reset VTP revision number before connecting any switch: change mode to transparent and back, or change VTP domain name and back
PREVENTION: Configure a VTP password on all switches in the domain
PREVENTION: Configure a VTP password on all switches in the domain
PREVENTION: Configure a VTP password on all switches in the domain

root_causes.cause: Newly connected switch from lab/storage has a higher VTP revision number from previous use in the same VTP domain
Switch was removed from the network, VLANs were modified (increasing revision), then re-connected — its higher revision overwrites production
VTPv1/v2 in server mode — no protection against higher revision numbers from unauthorized sources
No VTP password configured — any switch joining the domain name can propagate changes
VTP domain name inadvertently matches production domain on a lab or staging switch

failure_modes.scenario: VTP revision number bomb (VLAN database wipe)
symptoms: Multiple VLANs suddenly disappear from all switches in the domain
Mass connectivity loss as ports on deleted VLANs become inactive
'show vlan brief' shows only VLAN 1 and system VLANs
STP topology changes cascade across the network
'show vtp status' shows unexpectedly high revision number that matches the rogue switch

failure_modes.description: VLANs created on one switch do not appear on other switches in the VTP domain. New VLANs exist only locally despite VTP being configured.

resolutions.action: Verify VTP domain name matches on all switches: 'show vtp status'
Verify VTP domain name matches on all switches: 'show vtp status'
Verify VTP domain name matches on all switches: 'show vtp status'
Check VTP password: 'show vtp password' — must match on all switches
Check VTP password: 'show vtp password' — must match on all switches
Check VTP password: 'show vtp password' — must match on all switches
Ensure trunk links exist between switches: 'show interfaces trunk'
Ensure trunk links exist between switches: 'show interfaces trunk'
Ensure trunk links exist between switches: 'show interfaces trunk'
Verify VTP mode: switches in transparent mode do not propagate VLAN changes
Verify VTP mode: switches in transparent mode do not propagate VLAN changes
Verify VTP mode: switches in transparent mode do not propagate VLAN changes
Check VTP version: all switches must run the same VTP version or be compatible
Check VTP version: all switches must run the same VTP version or be compatible
Check VTP version: all switches must run the same VTP version or be compatible

root_causes.cause: VTP domain name mismatch between switches
VTP password mismatch — switch silently rejects VTP advertisements with wrong password
VTP mode set to transparent on the switch creating VLANs — transparent mode does not advertise VLAN changes
No trunk link between switches — VTP requires 802.1Q trunk to propagate
VTP version mismatch (v1 vs v2) — v1 cannot process v2 advertisements

symptoms: VLAN exists on one switch but not others
'show vtp status' shows different revision numbers on different switches
Ports on the missing VLAN are inactive on remote switches

messages.description: Contains VTP domain name, revision number, number of VLANs, and VTP version
key_fields: Number of Subset Advertisements to Follow
MD5 Digest (if VTP password is configured)
messages.name: Summary Advertisement
messages.when_sent: Every 5 minutes and immediately after a VLAN change

messages.description: Contains the actual VLAN database information
key_fields: VLAN Information (VLAN ID, Name, Type, MTU, State)
messages.name: Subset Advertisement
messages.when_sent: Following a summary advertisement after a VLAN change

purpose: VLAN Trunking Protocol — automatically propagates VLAN database changes across switches in the same VTP domain

states.description: Can create, modify, and delete VLANs. Advertises VLAN database to clients. Default mode.
states.name: SERVER

states.description: Cannot create, modify, or delete VLANs. Accepts and forwards VTP advertisements. Synchronizes VLAN database from servers.
states.name: CLIENT

states.description: Does not participate in VTP domain synchronization. Maintains its own VLAN database independently. Forwards VTP advertisements (VTPv2+) but does not apply them.
states.name: TRANSPARENT

states.description: VTP disabled entirely. Does not send, forward, or process VTP advertisements.
states.name: OFF

timers.default_rfc: 300s (5 minutes) for summary, triggered for subset
timers.name: VTP advertisement interval

notes.note: IOS-XE defaults to VTP server mode with domain name NULL. CRITICAL: Use VTPv3 or VTP transparent mode in production. VTPv3 adds primary server concept (must explicitly promote a server to primary with 'vtp primary'), preventing accidental VLAN database overwrites.

TRAP: To reset VTP revision number to 0 on IOS-XE, change the VTP domain name to something else and then change it back. Or switch to transparent mode and back. Simply rebooting does NOT reset the revision number. Always check 'show vtp status' before connecting any switch to the production network.
NX-OS defaults to VTP transparent mode. VTP is largely unnecessary on NX-OS deployments — VLANs are managed locally or via automation. If VTP is needed, enable with 'feature vtp'.
